title: StarCar yard snapshot - the wire contract
description: Spec 2026-07-23-yard-board S3: THE single owner of the server-to-browser wire shape (design rev 5 D15 - cross-language seam, schema-as-constructed-header). The Go server's structs and the browser's validator BOTH conform to this file; a hand-maintained mirror anywhere is a finding. Composition SEMANTICS (most-severe-wins, age quantisation) live in the spec and its vectors; this file owns SHAPE.

sseEventName.description: The SSE event name, exported here so server writer and client subscriber share ONE definition (design rev 5 section 5.4 item 4 - the one-character-apart failure mode is cross-language now). Tests on both sides assert against this constant.

register.description: The ONLY closed taxonomy (design rev 5 section 5.2). Severity order: nominal < in-progress < needs-attention. Growing this set is a constitution-level decision.

freshness.description: Closed by mechanism (design rev 5 section 5.2): an adapter is attached or not; if attached it has run or not; if run it succeeded or failed.

ageBucketMs.description: QUANTISED server-issued age (design rule 3: the client never computes age). Included in change detection.

boardCondition.description: A fault about the BOARD, rendered in chrome, distinct from yard status (design rev 5 section 6).

position.description: A PositionDef id from vocabularies.positions - open vocabulary, registry-owned (D11).

data.$comment: Shapes for the v0 lanes are pinned by $defs.trainsPayload, $defs.gatesPayload, and $defs.dispatchesPayload below (spec YB-5) - each landed red-first by the schema car (plan task 2.2). An unrecognised lane's payload is a free object the view meets with the no-renderer path, never a validation failure (Law 7); these three $defs are not $ref'd here because a lane's data shape is chosen by which lane it is, not by one closed schema union.
data.description: The lane payload. Shapes for the v0 lanes are pinned by the spec's payload contracts (S4) and their vectors; an unrecognised lane's payload is a free object the view meets with the no-renderer path, never a validation failure (Law 7).

trainsPayload.description: YB-5: the trains lane's data shape. Each car entry joins the WINNING manifest's member declaration with that subject's fold output; declaredNotObserved names members with no records (design S6).

id.description: The whole train: subject - the train identity (design DR3-3; never stripped of its prefix for meaning).

gate.description: Present when role is gate: names WHICH gate.

declaredNotObserved.description: Manifest members with no matching store records (design S6).

gatesPayload.description: YB-5: the gates lane's data shape.

outcome.description: VERBATIM - the gate's raw recorded outcome, never re-derived.

dispatchesPayload.description: YB-5: the dispatches lane's data shape - mirrors the fold's dispatch entry shape (schema/vectors/fold/ owns that shape) plus assigned: boolean per entry (yard inventory = unassigned, rendered loudly).

assigned.description: Yard inventory (unassigned) renders loudly, never silently.

seq.description: Monotonic, assigned AFTER change detection (design rev 5 section 5.6 carry of rev 3 rules). The client applies a snapshot only if seq exceeds the last applied.

asOf.description: Most recent SUCCESSFUL store scan; null only if none has ever succeeded.

heartbeatMs.description: Travels here so the client never hardcodes it; two missed heartbeats flip the client to disconnected.

storePathDisplay.description: CWD-relative or home-collapsed - NEVER a raw absolute path (normalisation law; this repo publishes screenshots).

laneCount.description: Completeness guard 3: 'registry declares N lanes' renders in chrome.

demoMode.description: True exactly when serving a demo fixture store (spec YB-15). Sole consumer: the view's persistent DEMO banner - a disclosure. No truth surface may branch on this field (gating-matrix; the design retired demo-mode suppression).

vocabularies.description: Presentational defs travel on EVERY snapshot so the view never hardcodes one and no separately fetched copy can drift (Law 6). Recognition VALUES stay owned by schema/vocab/*.json; these defs add label+register presentation. A value with no def renders by raw id through the detector path.

liveness.description: Defs for the fold's closed liveness states (dispatched/overdue/returned/presumed-lost) - the STATES are mechanism-closed; their presentation is still data.
